The classification of Lepigonum macrothecum is further narrowed down to the genus Lepigonum, which consists of several species found in New Caledonia. intro: Lepigonum macrothecum, also known as the Australian Desert Pea or Kurre, is a shrub native to the deserts of Western Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin infections, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lepigonum macrothecum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
